description: Catalog of AI tools that THIRD PARTIES use on you — your employer, doctor, HR, recruiter, interviewer. You're the data, not the user. This catalog documents what each tool does, who controls it, and what legal rights you have to object.

contexts.workplace: Tools your employer/colleagues may deploy on meetings, chat, email
contexts.hr-recruiting: Tools HR / recruiters use to analyze applications, interviews, performance
contexts.medical: Tools healthcare providers use on patient encounters
contexts.customer-service: Tools companies use on YOUR support calls
contexts.workforce-monitoring: Employer-installed desktop agents that capture mouse/keyboard/screen telemetry, often feeding AI-agent training pipelines. Always-on (not meeting-specific).

jurisdictions.EU: GDPR Article 22 + Article 21 (object to automated decision-making + profiling)
jurisdictions.US-federal: Limited — FTC guidance + ADA for AI in hiring
jurisdictions.US-state-IL: Illinois AI Video Interview Act (ADVID) — must consent to AI in interviews
jurisdictions.US-state-CA: CCPA + AB-331 (automated decision tools in employment)
jurisdictions.US-state-NY: NYC Local Law 144 — bias audit requirement for AEDTs
jurisdictions.HIPAA: US Federal — covered AI must be a Business Associate + HIPAA-compliant
jurisdictions.US-state-NY-EMA: NY Electronic Monitoring Act (2022) — requires written notice + acknowledgment before electronic monitoring begins
jurisdictions.US-state-IL-BIPA: Illinois Biometric Information Privacy Act — keystroke / mouse-movement patterns may qualify as 'biometric identifier' requiring informed written consent
jurisdictions.DE-works-council: Germany Betriebsverfassungsgesetz §87(1)(6) — monitoring tools require works council (Betriebsrat) co-determination
jurisdictions.EU-GDPR-art88: GDPR Article 88 — employment-context data processing must be limited by collective agreement and proportionality

zoom-ai-companion.whoDeploys: Any meeting host (requires their plan, applies to all participants)
zoom-ai-companion.notification: Zoom shows a banner when AI is active — but many users miss it or meetings are scheduled before you join
zoom-ai-companion.defaultBehavior: Host can enable without asking participants; participants see a notification
zoom-ai-companion.optOut: Can LEAVE the meeting. Cannot opt out while staying. Must be explicit to host.
zoom-ai-companion.notes: Zoom clarified 2023 that customer content is NOT used for third-party model training. AI Companion summaries stay within the customer account. Still: meeting transcripts are stored + searchable.

otter-ai.whoDeploys: Auto-joining bot — ANY Otter user can add it to a calendar invite
otter-ai.notification: Bot joins meeting as 'OtterPilot' — shows as participant
otter-ai.defaultBehavior: Training is opt-in by default (good); transcripts shared with inviter's team by default
otter-ai.optOut: Must ASK to remove bot. If you refuse recording, inviter must disable or everyone must leave.
otter-ai.notes: Otter also has `vanish ai-opt-out --otter` for your own Otter account's training toggle. This entry is for meetings OTHERS have Otter in.

fireflies-ai.notification: Bot appears as 'Fred' or similar named participant
fireflies-ai.defaultBehavior: Records by default; participants see bot but may not realize it's AI
fireflies-ai.optOut: Must ask host to kick the bot or decline meeting
fireflies-ai.notes: Fireflies has enterprise + free tiers. Sales-focused: transcripts get scored for 'buying signals' etc.

fathom.whoDeploys: Zoom extension — any Zoom user with Fathom can enable
fathom.notification: Zoom shows recording indicator; Fathom logo in the bot
fathom.defaultBehavior: Records when host enables; transcripts become searchable library
fathom.optOut: Leave the meeting or ask host to disable
fathom.notes: Fathom pitches 'free forever' tier to individuals — common in startups.

gong.whoDeploys: Enterprise sales orgs — typically auto-records ALL external-facing calls
gong.notification: Disclosure varies by company — many sales reps don't disclose unless asked
gong.defaultBehavior: Company-wide recording policy; employees often don't know what's captured
gong.optOut: Strongest when you're the CUSTOMER side — you can refuse and the sales rep must accommodate
gong.notes: If you're a buyer/customer on a sales call: the sales org is likely recording via Gong/Chorus. You have the right to refuse per your jurisdiction (2-party consent states in US). In EU under GDPR, silence is NOT consent.

chorus-zoominfo.defaultBehavior: Similar to Gong but with ZoomInfo data integration (pulls public data on your company)
chorus-zoominfo.optOut: As customer: refuse recording. As employee: company policy usually mandatory.

read-ai.whoDeploys: Auto-joining meeting bot; also Zoom/Gmail extensions
read-ai.notification: Bot joins named 'Read'; sends summary emails after
read-ai.defaultBehavior: Records by default; analyzes 'engagement' including facial expressions — more invasive than audio-only tools
read-ai.optOut: Ask host to remove. Disable camera to limit facial-analysis input.
read-ai.notes: Read.ai's facial-expression scoring is particularly controversial — implies scoring your 'engagement' without consent.

microsoft-teams-copilot.whoDeploys: Enterprise admins; per-meeting host can toggle
microsoft-teams-copilot.defaultBehavior: Enterprise-controlled — typically disabled by default in E3/E5 licenses; admins roll out selectively
microsoft-teams-copilot.optOut: Leave meeting. Can request admin-level exclusion but implementation varies.
microsoft-teams-copilot.notes: M365 Copilot enterprise data protection means the content is NOT used for model training — but IS stored within your org's tenant + admin-readable.

hirevue.notification: Candidate sees a recording consent screen
hirevue.defaultBehavior: Mandatory participation for roles using it — refusing may exclude you from the job
hirevue.optOut: Request live human interview instead (explicit accommodation request). Some jurisdictions require it.
hirevue.notes: HireVue discontinued face-analysis in 2021 after civil-rights criticism — still uses voice + response content. IL requires disclosure (AI Video Interview Act). NYC Local Law 144 requires annual bias audits.

pymetrics-harver.notification: Candidate completes 'games' — AI inference not usually disclosed
pymetrics-harver.optOut: Request alternative assessment (accommodation). Jurisdictions with ADA/disability law may require.
pymetrics-harver.notes: Pymetrics's personality-from-games approach has been criticized as pseudo-scientific. Candidates can request human-only evaluation.

abridge.notification: Patient usually informed verbally; consent forms vary by state
abridge.defaultBehavior: Many providers enable by default; patient must explicitly object
abridge.optOut: Right to decline under HIPAA + state law. Provider must accommodate human-only note-taking.
abridge.notes: Abridge is HIPAA-compliant Business Associate. Still: the conversation is analyzed by AI, audio may be retained for model improvement (check their BAA).

nuance-dax.whoDeploys: Large health systems (Mayo, Northwestern, Providence, etc.)
nuance-dax.notification: Varies by health system; often buried in intake paperwork
nuance-dax.optOut: Explicit request. Providers must offer alternative. HIPAA complaint possible if denied.
nuance-dax.notes: DAX captures AMBIENT audio during your visit — your voice, the doctor's, and anyone else in the room.

suki-ai.defaultBehavior: Opt-in typically required but implementation inconsistent

activtrak.whoDeploys: Employer IT — installed silently as a Windows/Mac service. No user-visible icon by default
activtrak.notification: Depends on employer policy. NY + DE + CT state law requires written notice; enforcement inconsistent
activtrak.defaultBehavior: Telemetry streamed to ActivTrak Cloud. In 2024+ tiers, data can feed 'Coach' AI recommendations + workflow-pattern models
activtrak.optOut: None for individual employee — employer controls deployment. Accommodation requires manager/HR/works-council escalation
activtrak.notes: ActivTrak markets itself as 'workforce analytics' rather than surveillance. Paid tiers (since 2024) add 'Coach AI' — workflow-modeling features that qualify as AI-training use under GDPR Art 22 + IL BIPA (keystroke biometrics).

teramind.whoDeploys: Employer IT — 'stealth mode' installs hide the agent from process list + tray. Full endpoint visibility
teramind.notification: Frequently deployed without worker notice despite most state laws requiring disclosure
teramind.defaultBehavior: Recorded video sessions + behavioral-analytics stream. 'Teramind AI' (2024+) does anomaly detection + behavior modeling
teramind.optOut: None for individuals. Opt-out requires employer-level configuration change or works-council intervention
teramind.notes: Teramind is the most-invasive tool in wide commercial use: full keylog + screen-record + file-transfer logging are standard features. Its 'anomaly detection' trains on your behavioral baseline — qualifies as AI-training use under GDPR Art 22 and likely BIPA in Illinois.

sources.label: Teramind — US state monitoring laws overview

hubstaff.whoDeploys: Employer or contracting client (common in agency/freelance workflows)
hubstaff.notification: User sees a tray icon + timer — more transparent than Teramind/Veriato, but AI-training use of captured data isn't usually disclosed
hubstaff.defaultBehavior: Streams screenshots + activity to Hubstaff Cloud. 2024+ 'Activity AI' classifies productivity patterns
hubstaff.optOut: Can pause the timer (but employer sees gap). Cannot fully opt-out while employed on that project
hubstaff.notes: Hubstaff is visible + relatively transparent compared to stealth tools — but the captured screenshots and activity data increasingly feed ML classifiers. Screenshot-interval data is arguably 'profiling' under GDPR Art 22.

time-doctor.notification: Tray icon visible. Similar to Hubstaff in transparency level
time-doctor.defaultBehavior: Screenshots + activity logs uploaded. 'Silent' mode (hides tray icon) is a paid feature some employers enable
time-doctor.optOut: Pause timer only. Silent-mode deployments have no user-visible way to pause
time-doctor.notes: Silent-mode deployments sidestep the transparency that normally differentiates Time Doctor from Teramind. If your employer uses silent mode without disclosure, that's typically unlawful in NY + DE + CT + EU.

insightful.notification: Variable — 'stealth install' is a documented feature
insightful.defaultBehavior: Upload to Insightful Cloud. 'Productivity AI' scoring is standard
insightful.notes: Insightful rebranded from Workpuls in 2022. Older installs may still use Workpuls paths. Stealth-install feature makes this one of the hardest to detect on endpoint without forensic tooling.

veriato.notification: Almost always stealth — Veriato markets itself for insider-threat investigations where notification would defeat the purpose
veriato.defaultBehavior: Continuous capture. 'Veriato AI' (since 2023) runs behavioral anomaly models on captured data
veriato.optOut: None — by design, the agent is not user-discoverable in default config
veriato.notes: Historically marketed for 'insider threat'. BIPA exposure is significant in Illinois — multi-million-dollar class actions have been filed against employers using keystroke-biometric tools without explicit written consent.

sources.label: JDSupra — BIPA class actions against keystroke-monitoring employers

interguard.defaultBehavior: Full endpoint capture streamed to dashboard. 2024+ ML-based 'data-loss prevention' and behavioral scoring
interguard.optOut: None — user generally cannot see or disable
interguard.notes: Awareness Technologies markets 'SONAR' (their DLP + analytics product) and InterGuard as a unified endpoint offering. Installation often named with randomized prefixes to resist user detection — the paths above catch the default install; if your employer uses custom-branded deployment, paths may differ.

microsoft-viva-insights.whoDeploys: M365 tenant admin (not installed locally — it's cloud-side analytics on your Office telemetry)
microsoft-viva-insights.notification: Delivered as Outlook add-in + weekly digest email. Most employees don't realize the aggregate analytics feeds manager dashboards
microsoft-viva-insights.defaultBehavior: Manager/team-level dashboards include individual-level metrics in some tiers. Copilot for 365 can be configured to train on org tenant data
microsoft-viva-insights.optOut: Individual setting: Outlook → Viva → 'Privacy' → Disable personal insights. Does NOT disable aggregate-into-manager-dashboard collection
microsoft-viva-insights.notes: Cloud-side primarily — local footprint is just the Outlook add-in. The actual concern is at the tenant level: M365 Copilot tenant admin settings control whether your email/chat becomes training data for your org's private Copilot model. 'Disable personal insights' does NOT disable tenant-level collection.

sources.label: Microsoft — 365 Copilot privacy / tenant data handling

dataCollected: unknown — the 'Meta memo' case: custom tooling your employer refuses to disclose
employer-internal.whoDeploys: Employer IT + internal AI/ML teams training workflow-modeling agents
employer-internal.notification: Typically none — internal tools don't have the disclosure obligations of third-party SaaS
employer-internal.defaultBehavior: Captured data feeds an internal LLM-training pipeline (the Meta + Salesforce 'agentic-AI from workforce telemetry' reports)
employer-internal.optOut: No public path. Must be pursued via data-subject-access-request (GDPR Art 15) or state-law equivalent
employer-internal.notes: This catalog entry covers the case where the Meta / Salesforce / similar reporting applies: your employer has built or commissioned an internal tool that's neither a commercial SKU nor disclosed to you. Use this flag to generate a disclosure-demand letter citing your DSAR rights — the employer must tell you what's being collected or explain why they won't.

sources.label: GDPR Article 15 — Right of access by the data subject

workplace-meeting.template:
To: [meeting organizer]

Subject: Request to disable AI recording/analysis in our meeting(s)

Hi [name],

I'm writing to formally request that AI-based transcription, recording, or analysis tools be disabled during meetings I attend. This includes tools such as {{toolNames}}.

My reasons:
1. I have not consented to my voice, likeness, or communication content being processed by third-party AI systems beyond the meeting itself.
2. {{jurisdictionClause}}
3. AI summaries risk misrepresenting my contributions — I prefer direct communication on the record.

I'm happy to participate in meetings that do NOT use AI tools. For meetings where the tool is enabled, please let me know in advance so I can decide whether to attend.

Thank you,
[Your name]

sales-call-recording.template:
To: [sales contact]

Subject: Recording consent for our upcoming call

Hi [name],

Before our call on [date], please confirm whether your team uses call-recording or AI-analysis tools (e.g., Gong, Chorus, ZoomInfo, Fathom) on external-facing sales conversations.

If so, I do NOT consent to recording or AI processing of my participation. {{jurisdictionClause}}

If recording is required by your company policy, please arrange for a call where it's disabled — otherwise I'll need to decline.

Thank you,
[Your name]

ai-interview.template:
To: [recruiter / hiring manager]

Subject: Request for human-conducted interview (not AI-mediated)

Hi [name],

I understand the {{companyName}} interview process includes an AI-assisted step using {{toolNames}}. I'm writing to formally request an alternative: a live interview conducted by human interviewers only.

{{jurisdictionClause}}

I'm a strong candidate and would appreciate being evaluated by people rather than algorithmic inference. Please let me know if an alternative path is available.

Thank you,
[Your name]

medical-ai.template:
To: [provider / clinic]

Subject: Request to decline AI transcription during my visits

Hi [provider/clinic],

I'm writing to decline use of {{toolNames}} (or any similar AI-based recording/transcription tool) during my medical visits.

Under HIPAA and my rights as a patient, I prefer that my conversations with my healthcare provider NOT be processed by AI tools, including but not limited to:
- Audio recording or transcription
- Retention of my voice or clinical conversations
- Third-party AI analysis, even under a BAA

{{jurisdictionClause}}

Please note this preference in my patient record and ensure it's honored across all my visits. I am happy to discuss alternative documentation methods (manual note-taking) with my provider.

Thank you,
[Your name]

workforce-monitoring-objection.template:
To: {{employerName}} — Human Resources + Legal + Data Protection Officer

Subject: Data subject request — disclosure + objection regarding workforce monitoring and AI training use

To whom it may concern,

I am submitting this as a formal written request with two components:

1. DISCLOSURE REQUEST

I request, in writing and within the statutory response window, the following information regarding monitoring software or systems deployed on my work device or on communication/collaboration services I use in the course of my employment with {{employerName}}:

  (a) The name and vendor of each monitoring system currently active on my device or account, including but not limited to any of the following that may be in use: {{toolNames}}.
  (b) The categories of data collected (including keystroke-rate data, mouse-movement telemetry, screen captures, application usage, file-system events, email/chat metadata or content, camera/microphone activation, location data, or biometric pattern data).
  (c) The retention period applicable to each category.
  (d) Whether any of this data is, has been, or may be used — directly or after de-identification or aggregation — to TRAIN, fine-tune, evaluate, or reinforce any artificial-intelligence model, AI agent, 'copilot', workflow-replication model, or behavioral-scoring system, whether operated by {{employerName}}, a subsidiary, a parent entity, or any third-party processor.
  (e) The identity of any third-party processors (including cloud vendors and AI-model providers) who receive this data.
  (f) The internal legal basis on which the monitoring is justified (e.g., business necessity, consent, legitimate interest), and the corresponding balancing test documentation.

{{detectedPaths}}

2. OBJECTION AND OPT-OUT

Separately from the disclosure request above, and regardless of whether the monitoring itself is permissible under applicable law, I hereby formally OBJECT to any use of data collected from me — whether on my work device, via my work accounts, or via my participation in meetings or chat — for purposes of TRAINING or IMPROVING any artificial-intelligence model, including but not limited to agentic-AI systems intended to replicate employee workflows.

Specifically, I object to:
- Inclusion of my keystrokes, mouse movements, screen captures, or activity patterns in any dataset used to train or fine-tune an AI agent.
- Use of my data in any automated decision-making or profiling that produces effects concerning me (employment evaluation, promotion, termination, compensation) without meaningful human review.

Legal basis for this objection:
{{jurisdictionClause}}

Additionally, and regardless of the primary jurisdiction cited above, I invoke:
  - GDPR Article 15 (right of access) for the disclosure portion of this request.
  - GDPR Article 21 (right to object to processing, including for direct marketing AND for processing based on legitimate interest).
  - GDPR Article 22 (right not to be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing, including profiling, which produces legal effects).
  - Any analogous rights under my jurisdiction's employment-privacy and data-protection statutes.

3. RESPONSE REQUESTED

Please respond in writing within the statutory response period applicable to my jurisdiction (typically 30 days under GDPR, 45 days under CCPA) confirming:
  (a) Receipt of this request.
  (b) Whether you will comply with the AI-training objection and, if so, the technical controls that will enforce it.
  (c) If you decline to comply, the specific legal basis for that refusal.

I reserve all rights, including the right to lodge a complaint with the relevant supervisory authority (my national DPA for GDPR matters; the relevant state AG or Labor Commissioner for US state-law matters; the works council / Betriebsrat where applicable).

Respectfully,
[Your name]
[Employee ID, if applicable]
[Date]

--
Sent via Vanish privacy toolkit — this request + any response is retained in a local HMAC-signed audit trail for evidentiary purposes.
